title: Axonopus mathewsii

intro: Axonopus mathewsii, commonly known as Mathews' carpet grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Brazil and other parts of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Axonopus mathewsii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Mathews' carpet grass is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is often employed in traditional medicine to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
